---
title: "Effect of Flood Zones on Cook County Properties"
author: "Alea Wilbur"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

<!---Figure out Township v City / Chicago township codes. Chicago Townships are 70-77 but pins don't start with a number above 33. --->

```{r setup, message = FALSE, warning=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(error = TRUE, echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)


library(tidyverse)
#install.packages("DBI")
#library(DBI)
library(data.table)
library(ggspatial)
library(gstat)
library(here)
library(httr)
library(jsonlite)
library(ptaxsim)
library(sf)
library(stars)
library(glue)


#remotes::install_gitlab("ccao-data-science---modeling/packages/ptaxsim")

#renv::install("gitlab::ccao-data-science---modeling/packages/ptaxsim")


# Create the DB connection with the default name expected by PTAXSIM functions
ptaxsim_db_conn <- DBI::dbConnect(RSQLite::SQLite(), "C:/Users/aleaw/OneDrive/Documents/PhD Fall 2021 - Spring 2022/Merriman RA/ptax/ptaxsim.db/ptaxsim-2022.0.0.db")

# has all potential property classes for pins
# downloaded from CCAO gitlab website
## I used this to merge additional information to the pins and class data later on.
class_dict <- read_csv("C:/Users/aleaw/OneDrive/Documents/PhD Fall 2021 - Spring 2022/Merriman RA/ptax/Necessary_Files/class_dict.csv")


setwd('C:/Users/aleaw/OneDrive/Documents/PhD Fall 2021 - Spring 2022/Merriman RA/ptax')

source('C:/Users/aleaw/OneDrive/Documents/PhD Fall 2021 - Spring 2022/Merriman RA/ptax/helper_tc_muninames.R')

muni_shp <- read_sf("https://gis.cookcountyil.gov/traditional/rest/services/politicalBoundary/MapServer/2/query?outFields=*&where=1%3D1&f=geojson")

```

```         
flood_fema_sfha = env_flood_fema_sfha
flood_fema_data_year = env_flood_fema_data_year,
flood_fs_factor = env_flood_fs_factor,
flood_fs_risk_direction = env_flood_fs_risk_direction,
flood_fs_data_year = env_flood_fs_data_year,
```

SFHA is the land area covered by the floodwaters of the “base flood” on flood insurance rate maps (FIRMs). The “base flood” is the flood having a 1% chance of being equaled or exceeded in any given year. This is the regulatory standard, also referred to as the “100-year flood”, and the SFHA is thus also referred to as the “100-year flood zone”. The base flood is the national standard used by the NFIP and all federal agencies for the purposes of requiring the purchase of flood insurance and regulating new development. Base Flood Elevation (BFE), which is the computed elevation to which floodwater is anticipated to rise during the base flood, is typically shown on FIRMs.

> FEMA FIRMs only include coastal and riverine flooding. They do NOT include Urban Flooding. Urban flooding is probably the one I am most interested in.

```{r pull-nbh-data}
base_url <- "https://datacatalog.cookcountyil.gov/resource/tx2p-k2g9.json"

nbh_pins <- GET(
  base_url,
  query = list(
    year = 2021,
   #  env_flood_fema_sfha = TRUE,
    `$select` = paste0(c("pin", "pin10", 
                         "class", "year",
                         "township_code", "township_name",
                         "nbhd_code", "census_puma_geoid",                                             "env_flood_fema_sfha","env_flood_fema_data_year",
                         "env_flood_fs_risk_direction", "env_flood_fs_factor",
                         "lat", "lon", 
                         "triad_name" ),
   collapse = ","),
   `$limit` = 500000000L
  )
)

nbh_pins <- fromJSON(rawToChar(nbh_pins$content))


```

```{r eval = FALSE, include = FALSE}
nbh_pins_2022 <- GET(
  base_url,
  query = list(
    year = 2022,
   #  env_flood_fema_sfha = TRUE,
    `$select` = paste0(c("pin", "pin10", 
                         "class", "year",
                         "township_code", "township_name",
                         "nbhd_code", "census_puma_geoid",                                             "env_flood_fema_sfha","env_flood_fema_data_year",
                         "env_flood_fs_risk_direction", "env_flood_fs_factor",
                         "lat", "lon", 
                         "triad_name" ),
   collapse = ","),
   `$limit` = 500000000L
  )
)

nbh_pins_2022 <- fromJSON(rawToChar(nbh_pins_2022$content))
```

Cook county assessor's office added FEMA floodplain data to its assessment models in 2019 [link](https://www.cookcountyassessor.com/news/floodplain-status-now-included-cook-county-property-assessments)

```{r}
flood_pins <- GET(
  base_url,
  query = list(
     env_flood_fema_sfha = TRUE,
    `$select` = paste0(c("year", "pin", "pin10", 
                         "class", 
                        "township_code", "township_name",
                         "nbhd_code", "census_puma_geoid",
                         "lat", "lon", 
                         #"triad_name" ,
                          "env_flood_fema_sfha","env_flood_fema_data_year",
                         "env_flood_fs_risk_direction", "env_flood_fs_factor"
                         ),
   collapse = ","),
   `$limit` = 500000000L
  )
)

# change data type to character
flood_pins2 <- fromJSON(rawToChar(flood_pins$content))$pin
#flood_pins2 %>% unique() # 29,000 + unique pins?

```

```{r eval = FALSE}
fema_year <- fromJSON(rawToChar(flood_pins$content))$env_flood_fema_data_year
# only has 2021 as a year
table(fema_year)

tax_year <-fromJSON(rawToChar(flood_pins$content))$year

table(tax_year) # only has 2021 and 2022

riskdirection <- fromJSON(rawToChar(flood_pins$content))$env_flood_fs_risk_direction

table(riskdirection)

# pin data for all years if in SFHA
flood_pins_ptax <- DBI::dbGetQuery(
  ptaxsim_db_conn,
  glue_sql(
  "SELECT*
  FROM pin
  WHERE pin IN ({flood_pins2*})
  ",
  .con = ptaxsim_db_conn
))

write_csv(flood_pins_ptax, "flood_pins_ptax.csv")
```

> Start with PINs in SFHAs that have existed for all years in PTAXSIM. Then examine the creation of new PINs over time.

Get all PINs in Cook County from tax year 2021 (dataframe named `pins_2021` and uses PTAXSIM `PIN` table in database):

```{r eval=FALSE}
pins_2021 <- DBI::dbGetQuery(
  ptaxsim_db_conn,
  glue_sql(
  "SELECT pin, year, class, tax_code_num
  FROM pin
  WHERE year = 2021
  ",
  .con = ptaxsim_db_conn
))
  
pins_2021 <- pins_2021 %>%
  mutate(SHFA = ifelse(pin %in% flood_pins_ptax$pin, 1, 0))


pins_2021 %>% 
  group_by(SHFA) %>%
  summarize(n())
```

```{r eval = FALSE, include = FALSE}
eq_factor <- DBI::dbGetQuery(
  ptaxsim_db_conn,
  glue_sql(
  "SELECT *
  FROM eq_factor
  ",
  .con = ptaxsim_db_conn
))

# write_csv(eq_factor, "eq_factor.csv")

tif_distribution <- DBI::dbGetQuery(
  ptaxsim_db_conn,
  glue_sql(
  "SELECT *
  FROM tif_distribution
  ",
  .con = ptaxsim_db_conn
))
# write_csv(tif_distribution, "tif_distribution.csv")

tif <- DBI::dbGetQuery(
  ptaxsim_db_conn,
  glue_sql(
  "SELECT *
  FROM tif
  ",
  .con = ptaxsim_db_conn
))
# write_csv(tif, "tif_start_stop.csv")

cpi <- DBI::dbGetQuery(
  ptaxsim_db_conn,
  glue_sql(
  "SELECT *
  FROM cpi
  ",
  .con = ptaxsim_db_conn
))
#write_csv(cpi, "cpi.csv")
```

# PINs and Parcels in Flood Plains (Based on PINs that existed in 2021)

-   29,545 unique pins at some point in time

-   23,262 unique parcels in cook county at some point in time

```{r}
flood_pins_ptax <- read_csv("flood_pins_ptax.csv")

flood_pins_ptax %>%  group_by(year) %>% summarize(count = n()) %>% arrange(year) %>%  ggplot()  + geom_line(aes(x=year, y = count)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(20000,30000), name = "PIN Count in SFHA") + theme_classic()
```

Examine PINs that existed every year:

```{r}
flood_pins_panel <- flood_pins_ptax %>% 
  group_by(pin) %>% 
  mutate(count = n()) %>% 
  filter(count > 15)

flood_pins_panel %>% group_by(year) %>% summarize(count = n()) %>% arrange(year)
```

```{r eval=FALSE}
unique_pins_in_fz <- flood_pins_ptax %>%  group_by(pin) %>% distinct(pin)



unique_parcels_in_fz <- flood_pins_ptax %>% 
  mutate(parcel_num = str_sub(pin, 1, 10)) %>%  
  group_by(parcel_num, year) %>% 
  summarize(parcels = n())
## 23,262 unique parcels in cook county at some point in time

table(unique_parcels_in_fz$year)
```

## Unique Blocks in Flood Zones

Since PINs can be divided over time, tracking a PIN's value over time is difficult. One attempt at dealing with this problem is to use the first 7 digits of the PIN to identify the block that the PIN is located in. Blocks (and the amount of land within the block) shouldn't change over time, and all PINs within that block begin with the same digits.

```{r summedValueInBlocks, eval=FALSE}

# summed values for 2021 only:
# mostly done to get unique blocks as a vector to go into the tax_bill() command in next chunk
blocks <- flood_pins_ptax %>% 
  filter(year == 2021) %>%
  mutate(major_class = str_sub(pin, 1,2),
    block_num = str_sub(pin, 1,7)) %>%
  group_by(block_num) %>%
  summarize(
    pincount = n(),
    av_mailed = sum(av_mailed),
    av_certified = sum(av_certified),
    av_board = sum(av_board),
    av_clerk = sum(av_clerk),
    class = first(class),
    major_class = first(major_class)
  )
blocks_strings <- blocks$block_num

blocks

```

> 3,460 Unique land Blocks have at least 1 PIN in the SFHA.(based on 2021 PINs with SFHA == 1)

### Pull PIN data for all pins within Blocks

Can pull assessed value for all PINs that begin with those 7 digits to measure changes at the Block level over time:

```{r AVforblockPINs, eval=FALSE}

# pin data for all years if in SFHA
flood_pins_ptax <- DBI::dbGetQuery(
  ptaxsim_db_conn,
  glue_sql(
  "SELECT*
  FROM pin
  WHERE substr(pin,1,7) IN ({blocks_strings*})
  ",
  .con = ptaxsim_db_conn
))

write_csv(flood_pins_ptax, "pins_in_Blocks_2006-2022.csv")
```

```{r}
flood_pins_ptax <- read_csv("pins_in_Blocks_2006-2022.csv")

flood_pins_ptax <- flood_pins_ptax %>% mutate(block_num = str_sub(pin, 1, 7),
                           parcel_num = str_sub(pin, 1, 10),
                           major_class = str_sub(class, 1, 1),
                           SFHA = ifelse(pin %in% flood_pins2, 1, 0))

block_sums <-  flood_pins_ptax %>% 
  group_by(year, block_num) %>%
  summarize(
    pincount = n(),
    av_mailed = sum(av_mailed),
    av_certified = sum(av_certified),
    av_board = sum(av_board),
    av_clerk = sum(av_clerk),
    class = first(class),
    class_low = min(class),
    class_high = max(class),
    major_class = first(major_class)
  )
  
```

## Per Neighborhood

```{r eval = FALSE}
NBHs <- read_sf("https://datacatalog.cookcountyil.gov/resource/pcdw-pxtg.geojson")

flood_pins_ptax %>% 
  filter(year == 2021 &SFHA == TRUE) %>%
  left_join(nbh_pins, by = "pin") %>%
  group_by(nbhd_code) %>% 
    summarize(count = n()) %>% 

  full_join(NBHs, by = c("nbhd_code" = "town_nbhd")) %>% 
  arrange(-count) %>%
  
 # full_join(nbh_shp, by = c("agency_name" = "AGENCY_DESC")) %>%
  ggplot(aes(fill = count)) + 
  geom_sf(aes(geometry = geometry), color = "black") + 
  labs(title = "Count of PINs per nbhd in SFHA", 
  caption = "") +
  theme_void() + 
  theme(axis.ticks = element_blank(), axis.text = element_blank())+# +#+
  scale_fill_steps2(
    high = "darkblue", low = "black",
  #  breaks = breaks_sd,  # tried to do breaks based on standard deviation but map does that in an easier way by setting the midpoint.
  na.value = "white",
  #  nice.breaks = FALSE,
    show.limits=TRUE,
    name = "Count",
   # labels = scales::
  )
```

![](images/clipboard-2024461645.png)

## Per Township

```{r}
township_shp <- read_sf("https://gis.cookcountyil.gov/traditional/rest/services/politicalBoundary/MapServer/3/query?outFields=*&where=1%3D1&f=geojson")

township_shp <-  township_shp  %>%
  mutate(name = str_to_title(NAME)) %>%
  mutate(name = case_when( 
    name == "North" ~ "North Chicago",
    name == "South" ~ "South Chicago",
    name == "West" ~ "West Chicago",
    .default = as.character(name)) 
  )

  
```

```{r}
#table(township_shp$name)
#table(nbh_pins$township_name)

flood_pins_ptax %>%
  filter(year == 2021 & SFHA = TRUE) %>%
  left_join(nbh_pins, by = "pin") %>%
   group_by(township_name) %>% 
    summarize(count = n())

flood_pins_ptax %>% 
  filter(year == 2020 & SFHA ==1) %>%
  left_join(nbh_pins, by = "pin") %>%
   group_by(township_name) %>% 
    summarize(count = n()) %>% 
  full_join(township_shp, by = c( "township_name" = "name") ) %>%
  arrange(-count) %>%
  ggplot(aes(fill = count)) + 
  geom_sf(aes(geometry = geometry), color = "black") + 
  labs(title = "Count of PINs per township in SFHA", 
  caption = "") +
  theme_void() + 
  theme(axis.ticks = element_blank(), axis.text = element_blank())+# +#+
  scale_fill_steps2(
    high = "darkblue", low = "black",
  na.value = "white",
  #  nice.breaks = FALSE,
    show.limits=TRUE,
    name = "Count",
   # labels = scales::
  )
```

> Not that Cicero has zero properties that are technically in a SFHA but is known for flooding (but it's URBAN flooding instead of riverine flooding. )

## Per Muni

```{r muni-pincount}

## Check wasn't doing join:

# flood_pins_ptax %>%
#   filter(year == 2021 & SFHA == 1) %>%
#   mutate(tax_code_num = as.character(tax_code_num)) %>%
#   left_join(tc_muninames, by = "tax_code_num") %>%
#   filter(!is.na(clean_name )) %>%
#   group_by(shpfile_name) %>% 
#   summarize(count = n()) %>% 
#   anti_join(muni_shp, b = c( "shpfile_name" = "MUNICIPALITY") )

# Everything joined 2/8/2024

flood_pins_ptax %>%
  filter(year == 2021 & SFHA == 1) %>%
  mutate(tax_code_num = as.character(tax_code_num)) %>%
  left_join(tc_muninames, by = "tax_code_num") %>%
  filter(!is.na(clean_name )) %>%
   group_by(shpfile_name) %>% 
    summarize(count = n()) %>% 
  full_join(muni_shp, b = c( "shpfile_name" = "MUNICIPALITY") ) %>%
arrange(-count) %>%
ggplot(aes(fill = count)) + 
  geom_sf(aes(geometry = geometry), color = "black") + 
  labs(title = "Count of PINs in SFHA, by Municipality", 
  caption = "") +
  theme_void() + 
  theme(axis.ticks = element_blank(), axis.text = element_blank())+# +#+
  scale_fill_steps2(
    high = "darkblue", low = "black",
  #  breaks = breaks_sd,  # tried to do breaks based on standard deviation but map does that in an easier way by setting the midpoint.
  na.value = "white",
  #  nice.breaks = FALSE,
    show.limits=TRUE,
    name = "Count",
  )
```

> Chunk below accidentally identified properties that change property class over time

*Note: These are for all PINs within a land block that had at least one PIN designated as SFHA.*

```{r}

flood_pins_ptax %>% 
  pivot_wider(id_cols = c(pin), names_from = "year", values_from =  "class") %>%
  mutate(change = as.numeric(`2021`)-as.numeric(`2006`)) %>% 
  filter(change !=0
         )

class_change <- flood_pins_ptax %>%   
  pivot_wider(id_cols = c(pin), names_from = "year", values_from =  "class") %>%
  mutate(change = as.numeric(`2021`)-as.numeric( `2006`)) %>% 
  filter(change == 1) %>%
  select(pin)




flood_pins_ptax %>% 
  mutate(class_1dig =str_sub(class, 1, 1)) %>%
  pivot_wider(id_cols = c(pin), names_from = "year", values_from =  "class_1dig") %>%
  mutate(change = as.numeric(`2021`)-as.numeric(`2006`)) %>% 
  filter(change !=0
         )

major_class_change <- flood_pins_ptax %>% 
    mutate(class_1dig =str_sub(class, 1, 1)) %>%

  pivot_wider(id_cols = c(pin), names_from = "year", values_from =  "class_1dig") %>%
  mutate(change = as.numeric(`2021`)-as.numeric(`2006`)) %>% 
  filter(change == 1) %>%
  select(pin)

```

```{r eval=FALSE}
flood_taxbills <- tax_bill(2008:2022, unique_pins_in_fz$pin, simplify = FALSE)

grouped_bills_pins <- flood_taxbills %>% #left_join(class_dict, by = c("class" = "class_code")) %>% # add major property types to pin data
  mutate(block_num = str_sub(pin, 1,7)) %>%
  group_by(block_num, pin, year) %>%
  summarize(taxing_agencies = n(),
            av = first(av),
            eav=first(eav),
            class = first(class),
            final_tax_to_dist = sum(final_tax_to_dist),
            final_tax_to_tif = sum(final_tax_to_tif),
            tax_amt_exe = sum(tax_amt_exe), # revenue lost due to exemptions
            tax_amt_pre_exe = sum(tax_amt_pre_exe), # total rev before all exemptions
            tax_amt_post_exe = sum(tax_amt_post_exe), # total rev after all exemptions
        rpm_tif_to_cps = sum(rpm_tif_to_cps), # not used
        rpm_tif_to_rpm = sum(rpm_tif_to_rpm), # not used
        rpm_tif_to_dist = sum(rpm_tif_to_dist), # not used
        tif_share = mean(tif_share), # not used
  ) %>%
  mutate(major_class = str_sub(class, 1,1),
         major_class_fct = case_when(
           major_class == '0' ~ "0 & 1 - Vacant and Exempt",
           major_class == '1' ~ "0 & 1 - Vacant and Exempt",
           major_class == '2' ~ "2 - Residential",
           major_class == '3' ~ "3 & 5 - Commercial",
           major_class == '5' ~ "3 & 5 - Commercial",
           major_class == '6' ~ "6, 7, 8 - Incentive",
           major_class == '7' ~ "6, 7, 8 - Incentive",
           major_class == '8' ~ "6, 7, 8 - Incentive",
           TRUE ~ "Other"
         ))


write_csv(grouped_bills_pins, "flood_taxbills_pins_in_blocks.csv")
```

```{r}
flood_taxbills <- read_csv("flood_taxbills_pins_in_blocks.csv")

grouped_bills <- flood_taxbills %>% #left_join(class_dict, by = c("class" = "class_code")) %>% # add major property types to pin data
  group_by(pin, year) %>%
  summarize(taxing_agencies = n(),
            av = first(av),
            eav=first(eav),
            class = first(class),
            final_tax_to_dist = sum(final_tax_to_dist),
            final_tax_to_tif = sum(final_tax_to_tif),
            tax_amt_exe = sum(tax_amt_exe), # revenue lost due to exemptions
            tax_amt_pre_exe = sum(tax_amt_pre_exe), # total rev before all exemptions
            tax_amt_post_exe = sum(tax_amt_post_exe), # total rev after all exemptions
        rpm_tif_to_cps = sum(rpm_tif_to_cps), # not used
        rpm_tif_to_rpm = sum(rpm_tif_to_rpm), # not used
        rpm_tif_to_dist = sum(rpm_tif_to_dist), # not used
        tif_share = mean(tif_share), # not used
  ) %>%
  mutate(major_class = str_sub(class, 1,1),
         major_class_fct = case_when(
           major_class == '0' ~ "0 & 1 - Vacant and Exempt",
           major_class == '1' ~ "0 & 1 - Vacant and Exempt",
           major_class == '2' ~ "2 - Residential",
           major_class == '3' ~ "3 & 5 - Commercial",
           major_class == '5' ~ "3 & 5 - Commercial",
           major_class == '6' ~ "6, 7, 8 - Incentive",
           major_class == '7' ~ "6, 7, 8 - Incentive",
           major_class == '8' ~ "6, 7, 8 - Incentive",
           TRUE ~ "Other"
         ))

grouped_bills <- grouped_bills %>% 
  mutate(township = str_sub(pin, 1, 2)) # %>%
 # filter(major_class %in% c(0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 9) & major_class_fct != "Other")

table(grouped_bills$township)
## Chicago Townships are not in the 70's like the 

twnshp_codes<-read_csv("township_codes.csv") %>% mutate(township_code = as.character(township_code))


grouped_bills <- grouped_bills %>% 
  left_join(twnshp_codes, by = c("township" = "township_code"))

table(grouped_bills$township)
table(grouped_bills$township_name)



```

> Ideally would have a variable for when properties were added to flood zone... FEMA data maybe? unsure.

```{r eval = FALSE, include = FALSE}
indexed_munis <- agency_data %>% ungroup() %>%
  filter(minor_type == "MUNI" ) %>%
  select( agency_name, year, total_non_cap_ext, total_capped_ext, cty_cook_eav, total_final_levy, home_rule_ind, Triad) %>%
  group_by(agency_name) %>% 
  arrange(year) %>%
  summarize(# Index values to 2006
    # added but not ran
    av_idx = (cty_cook_eav/cpi) / (cty_cook_eav[year==2006]/cpi),
    eav_idx = cty_cook_eav / cty_cook_eav[year == 2006] ,
    levy_idx = total_final_levy / total_final_levy[year == 2006] ,
    cappedext_idx = total_capped_ext / total_capped_ext[year == 2006] ,
    noncappedext_idx = total_non_cap_ext / total_non_cap_ext[year == 2006] ,
    year = year, 
    home_rule_ind = home_rule_ind,  
    Triad = Triad) 

endpoints <- indexed_munis %>% filter(year == 2021)

indexed_munis %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x=year, y = levy_idx, group = agency_name, label = agency_name, color = as.factor(home_rule_ind))) + 
    geom_line(alpha=.5, linetype = "dashed") +
    geom_text(data = endpoints, aes(x = year, y = levy_idx, label = agency_name), size = 2, vjust=-1.5, check_overlap = TRUE)+

  geom_point() + 
  theme_classic()+
  labs(title = "For comparison: Municipalities' Total Final Levy Indexed to 2006")
```

### Assessed Value over Time

```{r av-graphs}
grouped_bills grouped_bills_pins

grouped_bills %>% 
  group_by(year) %>%
  summarize(pins_per_year = n(),
            av = sum(av),
            eav = sum(eav),
            eav_per_pin = eav/pins_per_year,
            av_per_pin = av/pins_per_year) %>%
  ggplot() + 
  geom_line(aes(x = year, y = eav_per_pin)) +
  geom_line(aes(x = year, y = av_per_pin
               ), color = "blue")+
  labs(title = "EAV and AV per PIN")


grouped_bills %>% 
  group_by(year, major_class_fct) %>%
  summarize(pins_per_year = n(),
            av = sum(av),
            eav = sum(eav),
            eav_per_pin = eav/pins_per_year,
            av_per_pin = av/pins_per_year) %>%
  ggplot() + 
 # geom_line(aes(x = year, y = eav_per_pin, group = major_class_fct, color = major_class_fct) )+
  geom_line(aes(x = year, y = av_per_pin, group = major_class_fct, color = major_class_fct) )


grouped_bills %>% 
  group_by(year, major_class_fct) %>%
  summarize(pins_per_year = n(),
            av = sum(av),
            eav = sum(eav),
            eav_per_pin = eav/pins_per_year,
            av_per_pin = av/pins_per_year) %>%
  ggplot() + 
  geom_line(aes(x = year, y = av, group = major_class_fct, color = major_class_fct) )
 # geom_line(aes(x = year, y = eav, group = major_class_fct, color = major_class_fct) )


grouped_bills %>% 
  filter(year %in% c(2016, 2017, 2018, 2019, 2020, 2021)) %>%
  group_by(year, major_class_fct) %>%
  summarize(pins_per_year = n(),
            av = sum(av),
            eav = sum(eav),
            eav_per_pin = eav/pins_per_year,
            av_per_pin = av/pins_per_year) %>%
  ggplot() + 
  geom_line(aes(x = year, y = av, group = major_class_fct, color = major_class_fct) ) + 
  labs(title = "Potential Policy Change in 2018?", subtitle="Assessor Office began using flood risk in assessments?" )


grouped_bills %>% 
  filter(year %in% c(2016, 2017, 2018, 2019, 2020, 2021)) %>%
  group_by(year, major_class_fct) %>%
  summarize(pins_per_year = n(),
            av = sum(av),
            eav = sum(eav),
            eav_per_pin = eav/pins_per_year,
            av_per_pin = av/pins_per_year) %>%
  ggplot() + 
  geom_line(aes(x = year, y = av_per_pin, group = major_class_fct, color = major_class_fct) ) + 
  labs(title = "Potential Policy Change in 2018?", subtitle="Assessor Office began using flood risk in assessments?" )


grouped_bills %>% 
  group_by(year, major_class_fct) %>%
  summarize(pins_per_year = n(),
            eav = sum(eav),
            eav_per_pin = eav/pins_per_year) %>%
  ggplot() + geom_line(aes(x = year, y = pins_per_year, group = major_class_fct, color = major_class_fct) )

```

```{r floodpin-graphs-av}

flood_pins_ptax<-flood_pins_ptax %>% 
    mutate(major_class = str_sub(class, 1,1),
         major_class_fct = case_when(
           major_class == '0' ~ "0 & 1 - Vacant and Exempt",
           major_class == '1' ~ "0 & 1 - Vacant and Exempt",
           major_class == '2' ~ "2 - Residential",
           major_class == '3' ~ "3 & 5 - Commercial",
           major_class == '5' ~ "3 & 5 - Commercial",
           major_class == '6' ~ "6, 7, 8 - Incentive",
           major_class == '7' ~ "6, 7, 8 - Incentive",
           major_class == '8' ~ "6, 7, 8 - Incentive",
           TRUE ~ "Other"
         ))
  

flood_pins_ptax %>%  group_by(year) %>%
  summarize(pins_per_year = n(),
            av_mailed = sum(av_mailed),
            av_certified = sum(av_certified),
            av_board = sum(av_board),
            av_clerk = sum(av_clerk),
          #  eav = sum(eav),
           # eav_per_pin = eav/pins_per_year,
            #av_per_pin = av/pins_per_year
          ) %>%
  ggplot() + 
  geom_line(aes(x = year, y = av_mailed)) +
  geom_line(aes(x = year, y = av_clerk
               ), color = "blue")+
    geom_line(aes(x = year, y = av_certified
               ), color = "green")+
    geom_line(aes(x = year, y = av_board
               ), color = "purple")+
  labs(title = "Mailed AV and Clerk AV", caption = "Black is mailed, blue is Clerk, green is certified, purple is Board")


flood_pins_ptax %>% 
  filter(major_class == 2) %>%
  group_by(year, major_class_fct) %>%
  summarize(pins_per_year = n(),
            av_mailed = sum(av_mailed),
            av_clerk = sum(av_clerk),
            mailed_per_pin = av_mailed/pins_per_year,
            clerk_per_pin = av_clerk / pins_per_year) %>%
  ggplot() + 
 geom_line(aes(x = year, y = mailed_per_pin, group = major_class_fct, color = major_class_fct) )+
   geom_line(aes(x = year, y = clerk_per_pin), color = "blue")
 # geom_line(aes(x = year, y = av_per_pin) )


```

```{r eav-graphs, eval=FALSE, include = FALSE}

grouped_bills %>% 
  group_by(year) %>%
  summarize(pins_per_year = n(),
            av = sum(av),
            eav = sum(eav),
            eav_per_pin = eav/pins_per_year,
            av_per_pin = av/pins_per_year) %>%
  ggplot() + 
  geom_line(aes(x = year, y = eav_per_pin)) +
  geom_line(aes(x = year, y = av_per_pin
               ))


grouped_bills %>% 
  group_by(year, major_class_fct) %>%
  summarize(pins_per_year = n(),
            av = sum(av),
            eav = sum(eav),
            eav_per_pin = eav/pins_per_year,
            av_per_pin = av/pins_per_year) %>%
  ggplot() + geom_line(aes(x = year, y = eav_per_pin, group = major_class_fct, color = major_class_fct) )


grouped_bills %>% 
  group_by(year, major_class_fct) %>%
  summarize(pins_per_year = n(),
            av = sum(av),
            eav = sum(eav),
            eav_per_pin = eav/pins_per_year,
            av_per_pin = av/pins_per_year) %>%
  ggplot() + geom_line(aes(x = year, y = eav, group = major_class_fct, color = major_class_fct) )


grouped_bills %>% 
  filter(year %in% c(2016, 2017, 2018, 2019, 2020)) %>%
  group_by(year, major_class_fct) %>%
  summarize(pins_per_year = n(),
            av = sum(av),
            eav = sum(eav),
            eav_per_pin = eav/pins_per_year,
            av_per_pin = av/pins_per_year) %>%
  ggplot() + geom_line(aes(x = year, y = eav, group = major_class_fct, color = major_class_fct) ) + labs(title = "Potential Policy Change in 2018?", subtitle="Assessor Office began using floor risk in assessments" )



grouped_bills %>% 
  group_by(year, major_class_fct) %>%
  summarize(pins_per_year = n(),
            eav = sum(eav),
            eav_per_pin = eav/pins_per_year) %>%
  ggplot() + geom_line(aes(x = year, y = pins_per_year, group = major_class_fct, color = major_class_fct) )



grouped_bills  %>% group_by(year) %>% summarize(count = n()) %>% 
  arrange(count) %>% ggplot()+ geom_line(aes(x=year, y=count)) + 
  labs(title = "Cook County Pins in Flood Plains", 
       caption = "Data from Cook County Data Portal")
```

## Mapping Parcels

Example Link: [<https://ccao-data.github.io/ptaxsim/articles/mapping.html>]

At the 10 digit pin number, there are 23,332 pins.

-   Last 4 digits of the 14 digit pin are for indicating the unit number within a building. Single family homes with only one tax payer end with 0000; condos with more than one unit end with additional identifiers. The 10-digit pin corresponds to a geographic location of the building.

Pin geometries for pins with flood zone SFHA status.

> make smaller maps. Either by Triad or township or something else smaller than Cook County.

```{r}
#install.packages("prettymapr")
library(prettymapr)


flood_pin10s <- unique(substr(flood_pins2, 1, 10))

flood_pins_geo <- lookup_pin10_geometry(year = 2021, pin10 = flood_pin10s) %>%
  st_as_sf(wkt = "geometry", crs = 4326)

flood_pins_geo <- flood_pins_geo %>%
  left_join(
    flood_pins_ptax %>%
      mutate(pin10 = substr(pin, 1, 10)) %>%
      group_by(pin10) %>%
      summarize(class = first(class)),
    by = "pin10"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    major_class = substr(class, 1, 1),
    township = str_sub(as.character(pin10), 1, 2),
    major_class_fct = recode_factor(
      major_class,
      "0" = "0 & 1 - Vacant and Exempt",
      "1" = "0 & 1 - Vacant and Exempt",
      "2" = "2 - Residential",
      "3" = "3 & 5 - Commercial",
      "5" = "3 & 5 - Commercial",
      "6" = "6, 7, 8 - Incentive",
      "7" = "6, 7, 8 - Incentive",
      "8" = "6, 7, 8 - Incentive"
    )
  )
table(flood_pins_geo$major_class)

twnshp_codes$township_code <- as.character(twnshp_codes$township_code)
flood_pins_geo <- flood_pins_geo %>% 
  left_join(twnshp_codes, by =c("township" = "township_code"))

table(flood_pins_geo$township_name)
table(flood_pins_geo$triad_code)

table(flood_pins_geo$township)



# add border for cook County
cook_border <- read_sf("https://gis.cookcountyil.gov/traditional/rest/services/plss/MapServer/1/query?outFields=*&where=1%3D1&f=geojson")
```

```{r eval = FALSE}
flood_pins_map_all <- ggplot() +
  annotation_map_tile(type = "cartolight", zoomin = -1) +
  geom_sf(
    data = flood_pins_geo,
    aes(fill = major_class_fct),
    alpha = 0.5,
    linewidth = 0.1
  ) +
 geom_sf(data = cook_border, fill = "transparent", linewidth = 1) +
  annotation_scale(location = "br") +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "",
    values = c(
      "0 & 1 - Vacant and Exempt" = "#984ea3",
      "2 - Residential" = "#e41a1c",
      "3 & 5 - Commercial" = "#377eb8",
      "6, 7, 8 - Incentive" = "#4daf4a"
    )
  ) +
  guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 5))) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(
    legend.title = element_text(size = 14),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.key.size = unit(18, "points"),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )

flood_pins_map_all
```

```{r eval=FALSE}
top5 <- c("05")
flood_pins_map_north <- ggplot() +
  annotation_map_tile(type = "cartolight", zoomin = -1) +
  geom_sf(
    data = flood_pins_geo %>% filter(township %in% top5),
    aes(fill = major_class_fct),
    alpha = 0.5,
    linewidth = 0.1
  ) +
#  geom_sf(data = cook_border, fill = "transparent", linewidth = 1) +
  annotation_scale(location = "br") +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "",
    values = c(
      "0 & 1 - Vacant and Exempt" = "#984ea3",
      "2 - Residential" = "#e41a1c",
      "3 & 5 - Commercial" = "#377eb8",
      "6, 7, 8 - Incentive" = "#4daf4a"
    )
  ) +
  guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 5))) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(
    legend.title = element_text(size = 14),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.key.size = unit(18, "points"),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  ) + facet_wrap(~township, # scales = "free",
                # nrow = 5,
                 shrink = FALSE)

flood_pins_map_north
```

#### New Trier

```{r eval=FALSE}
grouped_bills %>% filter(township == 05) %>% 
  group_by(year, major_class_fct) %>%
  summarize(pins_per_year = n(),
            av = sum(av),
            eav = sum(eav),
            eav_per_pin = eav/pins_per_year,
            av_per_pin = av/pins_per_year) %>%  ggplot() +
  geom_line(aes(x=year, y=av_per_pin, color = major_class_fct))


grouped_bills %>% filter(township == 08) %>% 
  group_by(year, major_class_fct) %>%
  summarize(pins_per_year = n(),
            av = sum(av),
            eav = sum(eav),
            eav_per_pin = eav/pins_per_year,
            av_per_pin = av/pins_per_year) %>%  ggplot() +
  geom_line(aes(x=year, y=av_per_pin, color = major_class_fct))


grouped_bills %>% filter(township == 10) %>% 
  group_by(year, major_class_fct) %>%
  summarize(pins_per_year = n(),
            av = sum(av),
            eav = sum(eav),
            eav_per_pin = eav/pins_per_year,
            av_per_pin = av/pins_per_year) %>%  ggplot() +
  geom_line(aes(x=year, y=av_per_pin, color = major_class_fct)) +
  geom_vline(aes(xintercept= (2009))) +
  geom_vline(aes(xintercept= (2012))) +
  geom_vline(aes(xintercept= (2015))) +
    geom_vline(aes(xintercept= (2018)))

  
```

New Trier had corrected bills mailed out in 2019 when the assessor began using floodplain data. New Trier is township #23 in Cook County township codes. New Trier is in the North Triad, up by Glencoe.

> Pull first round assessments and then corrected assessments!

```{r NewTrier}
townships <- c("08")

grouped_bills %>% 
  filter(township %in% c("23")) %>% 
  group_by(year, major_class_fct) %>%
  summarize(pins_per_year = n(),
            av = sum(av),
            eav = sum(eav),
            eav_per_pin = eav/pins_per_year,
            av_per_pin = av/pins_per_year) %>%  ggplot() +
  geom_line(aes(x=year, y=av_per_pin, color = major_class_fct)) +
  geom_vline(aes(xintercept= (2019))) +
  labs(title = "New Trier assessments",
       subtitle = "New information in 2019")

flood_pins_map_north <- ggplot() +
  annotation_map_tile(type = "cartolight", zoomin = -1) +
  geom_sf(
    data = flood_pins_geo %>% filter(township %in% townships),
    aes(fill = major_class_fct),
    alpha = 0.5,
    linewidth = 0.1
  ) +
 # geom_sf(data = cook_border, fill = "transparent", linewidth = 1) +
  annotation_scale(location = "br") +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "",
    values = c(
      "0 & 1 - Vacant and Exempt" = "#984ea3",
      "2 - Residential" = "#e41a1c",
      "3 & 5 - Commercial" = "#377eb8",
      "6, 7, 8 - Incentive" = "#4daf4a"
    )
  ) +
  guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 5))) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(
    legend.title = element_text(size = 14),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.key.size = unit(18, "points"),
    legend.position = "bottom" 
  ) + labs(title = "New Trier Parcels in SFHA")

flood_pins_map_north
```

```{r eval=FALSE}
townships <- c("28", "29")


flood_pins_map_south <- ggplot() +
  annotation_map_tile(type = "cartolight", zoomin = -1) +
  geom_sf(
    data = flood_pins_geo %>% filter(township %in% townships),
    aes(fill = major_class_fct),
    alpha = 0.5,
    linewidth = 0.1
  ) +
 # geom_sf(data = cook_border, fill = "transparent", linewidth = 1) +
  annotation_scale(location = "br") +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "",
    values = c(
      "0 & 1 - Vacant and Exempt" = "#984ea3",
      "2 - Residential" = "#e41a1c",
      "3 & 5 - Commercial" = "#377eb8",
      "6, 7, 8 - Incentive" = "#4daf4a"
    )
  ) +
  guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 5))) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(
    legend.title = element_text(size = 14),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.key.size = unit(18, "points"),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )
  

flood_pins_map_south

grouped_bills %>% filter(township %in% c("28", "29")) %>% 
  group_by(year, major_class_fct) %>%
  summarize(pins_per_year = n(),
            av = sum(av),
            eav = sum(eav),
            eav_per_pin = eav/pins_per_year,
            av_per_pin = av/pins_per_year) %>%  ggplot() +
  geom_line(aes(x=year, y=av_per_pin, color = major_class_fct)) +
  geom_vline(aes(xintercept= (2010))) +
  geom_vline(aes(xintercept= (2013))) +
  geom_vline(aes(xintercept= (2016))) +
    geom_vline(aes(xintercept= (2019)))
```

```{r Cicero}
townships <- c("15")


flood_pins_map_south <- ggplot() +
  annotation_map_tile(type = "cartolight", zoomin = -1) +
  geom_sf(
    data = flood_pins_geo %>% filter(township %in% townships),
    aes(fill = major_class_fct),
    alpha = 0.5,
    linewidth = 0.1
  ) +
 # geom_sf(data = cook_border, fill = "transparent", linewidth = 1) +
  annotation_scale(location = "br") +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "",
    values = c(
      "0 & 1 - Vacant and Exempt" = "#984ea3",
      "2 - Residential" = "#e41a1c",
      "3 & 5 - Commercial" = "#377eb8",
      "6, 7, 8 - Incentive" = "#4daf4a"
    )
  ) +
  guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 5))) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(
    legend.title = element_text(size = 14),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.key.size = unit(18, "points"),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )
  

flood_pins_map_south

grouped_bills %>% filter(township %in% c("15")) %>% 
  group_by(year, major_class_fct) %>%
  summarize(pins_per_year = n(),
            av = sum(av),
            eav = sum(eav),
            eav_per_pin = eav/pins_per_year,
            av_per_pin = av/pins_per_year) %>%  ggplot() +
  geom_line(aes(x=year, y=av_per_pin, color = major_class_fct)) +
  geom_vline(aes(xintercept= (2010))) +
  geom_vline(aes(xintercept= (2013))) +
  geom_vline(aes(xintercept= (2016))) +
    geom_vline(aes(xintercept= (2019)))
```

```{r eval=FALSE}
flood_pins_map_chi <- ggplot() +
  annotation_map_tile(type = "cartolight", zoomin = -1) +
  geom_sf(
    data = flood_pins_geo,
    aes(fill = major_class_fct),
    alpha = 0.5,
    linewidth = 0.1
  ) +
  geom_sf(data = cook_border, fill = "transparent", linewidth = 1) +
  annotation_scale(location = "br") +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "",
    values = c(
      "0 & 1 - Vacant and Exempt" = "#984ea3",
      "2 - Residential" = "#e41a1c",
      "3 & 5 - Commercial" = "#377eb8",
      "6, 7, 8 - Incentive" = "#4daf4a"
    )
  ) +
  guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 5))) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(
    legend.title = element_text(size = 14),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.key.size = unit(18, "points"),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )

```

```{r}
#save.image("flood_pins_map.pdf")
```
